document.title: FCC Partial Test Report
standards: FCC KDB 789033 D02 General UNII Test Procedures New Rules v01r04
FCC KDB 644545 D03 Guidance for IEEE 802 11ac New Rules v01
FCC KDB 662911 D01 Multiple Transmitter Output v02r01
FCC KDB 412172 D01 Determining ERP and EIRP v01r01

sourceSummary.oneLine: FCC partial test report for Laird Technologies ST60-2230C-P 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module under 47 CFR FCC Part 15.407.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports FCC compliance evidence for radiated and band edge emissions testing of the identified wireless module.
keyPoints: Report No. FR740701-02AN, Report Version Rev. 01, Initial issue Nov. 29, 2017.
FCC ID is SQG-60SIPT and the equipment is an 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module.
International Certification Corp. declared the tested sample evaluated and in compliance with the requirement of the stated standard.
Summary of test results lists Radiated Emissions under FCC Rules 15.407(b) and 15.209 as Pass.
Testing applied standards include 47 CFR FCC Part 15.407, ANSI C63.10-2013, and multiple FCC KDB procedures.

evidence.claim: The document is an FCC Partial Test Report.
The report number is FR740701-02AN and the report version is Rev. 01.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: FR740701-02AN Page : 1 of 49 Report Version: Rev. 01

evidence.claim: The report was initially issued on Nov. 29, 2017.
evidence.evidenceText: Release Record Report No. Version Description Issued Date FR740701-02AN Rev. 01 Initial issue Nov. 29, 2017

evidence.claim: The tested equipment is an 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module.
The model number identified on the report cover is ST60-2230C-P.
The EUT product details list models ST60-2230C-P and SU60-2230C-P.
evidence.evidenceText: The following models are provided to this EUT. Brand Name Model Name Product Name Description Laird Technologies ST60-2230C-P 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module For marketing purpose SU60-2230C-P

evidence.claim: The applicant and brand name are Laird Technologies.
evidence.evidenceText: Brand Name : Laird Technologies Applicant : Laird Technologies

evidence.claim: The stated standard on the report cover is 47 CFR FCC Part 15.407.
The sample was declared evaluated and in compliance with the requirement of the stated standards by International Certification Corp.
evidence.evidenceText: We, International Certification Corp., would like to declare that the tested sample has been evaluated and in compliance with the requirement of the above standards.

evidence.claim: The summary of test results lists radiated emissions under FCC Rules 15.407(b) and 15.209 as Pass.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary of Test Results FCC Rules Test Items Measured Result 15.407(b) 15.209 Radiated Emissions [dBuV/m at 3m]: 5150.00MHz 52.94 (Margin -1.06dB) - AV Pass

evidence.claim: The received date was Sep. 27, 2017 and the tested date was Oct. 02 to Oct. 03, 2017.
evidence.evidenceText: Received Date : Sep. 27, 2017 Tested Date : Oct. 02 ~ Oct. 03, 2017

evidence.claim: The EUT power supply type is DC 3.3V from host.
evidence.evidenceText: Power Supply Type DC 3.3V from host

evidence.evidenceText: Ant. No. Model Type Connector Operating Frequencies (MHz) / Antenna Gain (dBi) ... 1 Laird NanoBlade-IP04 PCB Dipole IPEX U.FL

evidence.claim: Testing condition for radiated emissions used test site 03CH03-WS with ambient condition 22-24°C / 66-67% and tested by Aska Huang.
evidence.evidenceText: Test Item Test Site Ambient Condition Tested By Radiated Emissions 03CH03-WS 22-24°C / 66-67% Aska Huang

evidence.claim: Measurement uncertainty is ±3.66 dB for radiated emission at or below 1 GHz and ±5.37 dB for radiated emission above 1 GHz.
evidence.evidenceText: Measurement Uncertainty Parameters Uncertainty Radiated emission ≤ 1GHz ±3.66 dB Radiated emission > 1GHz ±5.37 dB

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata productSignal is Unclassified, but the source text identifies product models ST60-2230C-P and SU60-2230C-P.

Detailed numeric tables or plots for many transmitter radiated unwanted emissions pages are not present in the extracted text; extraction relies on the cover, summary, limits, procedures, and readable notes.
